content.problemStatement:
The exciting World Cup 2010's summer is coming! And Johnny's family shop is busy preparing a colorful lighting board for the summer.

The board has a rectangular shape of size M x N. There are certain positions on the board which Johnny wants to put in light bulbs.

Johnny wants to decorate the board by colorful light bulbs. To make the board look attractive, he wants the bulbs in the same column or row to have different colors! On the other hand, to preserve the harmony look, Johnny doesn't want the bulbs to have many different colors.

Write a program to help Johnny decorate the boards in such a way that the number of color used is minimum.
constraints: Each character in the board is either '0' or '1'
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers M and N on the first line, followed by M lines of N characters each.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of colors used on the first line, followed by M lines of N integers each, representing the color assigned to each cell.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The minimum number of colors required is 4. The colors assigned to each cell are shown in the output.
The minimum number of colors required is 3. The colors assigned to each cell are shown in the output.
The minimum number of colors required is 2. The colors assigned to each cell are shown in the output.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by representing the board as a graph, where each cell is a node, and two nodes are connected if they are in the same row or column. We then use a greedy algorithm to assign colors to each node, ensuring that no two adjacent nodes have the same color.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M * N * (M + N)) per test case, O(T * M * N * (M + N)) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M * N) — for the graph and color assignments.
